title: lycopodiella diffusa
intro: Lycopodiella diffusa, also known as the spreading bog spike-moss, is a type of low-growing, perennial plant that belongs to the club moss family. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe and calm irritated skin and mucous membranes. The health benefits of Lycopodiella diffusa include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and support the respiratory system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used in teas, infusions, or topical creams and ointments to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
